title: Charred Polish Cheddar Cheese Gratin
description: A classic Polish dish featuring a layer of cheddar cheese, bread crumbs, and a sprinkle of paprika, all topped with a crispy crust.
instructions: Shred the cheddar cheese and set aside.
In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until caramelized, about 15 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the bread crumbs and stir to combine with the onion mixture.
In a 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of the bread crumb mixture.
Top with the shredded cheddar cheese and sprinkle with paprika.
Place the baking dish under the broiler and char the top until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
Remove from oven and let cool slightly before placing under an ice bath to stop cooking.
Serve warm, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or thyme, if desired.
